mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically boosting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 5.5% within a single month, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that compress consumer spending and corporate revenue growth, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE exceeds 5.5% in a monthly reading
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE crosses 5.5%, or the signal shows no predictive power in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in industrial production and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in real economic activity and credit-sensitive demand, which compresses equity risk premiums and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict returns with a Rank IC above 0.05 over a 3-year rolling window, or individual series outperform the combined signal in directional accuracy.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid monetary tightening, rising short-term rates amplify discount rate pressures on equity valuations, producing strongly negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises > 0.25pp in a single month
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive or neutral in over 50% of triggered instances, or the signal's predictive power vanishes when controlling for equity volatility.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real personal consumption expenditures (proxied by RSAFS) reflects robust household balance sheets and aggregate demand, which supports corporate earnings revisions and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: The signal exhibits a negative or zero Rank IC over any 12-month rolling window, or forward returns do not statistically differ from the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: When the industrial production index falls below 95.0, it indicates a supply-side bottleneck reversal or capacity utilization drop that typically precedes earnings downgrades, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not significantly lower than baseline when INDPRO breaches the threshold, or the signal generates excessive false positives in stable production environments.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising unemployment and elevated inflation (proxied by CPI) creates a stagflationary environment that forces the Federal Reserve to prioritize price stability over employment, compressing equity valuations and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction term fails to improve out-of-sample prediction accuracy over a single-series model, or forward returns are not systematically negative when both series move adversely.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion where housing starts peak and GDP growth decelerates, the lagged effect of monetary policy transmission finally cracks consumer credit demand, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth rate falls below 1.5%
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive or neutral in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the signal's predictive power is fully absorbed by volatility measures.
